title: Cyperus remotus
taxonomy: Cyperus remotus is classified as a species of sedge in the family Cyperaceae. This classification places it within the monocotyledonous flowering plants. Cyperus remotus is further categorized as a member of the genus Cyperus, which includes approximately 900 species of sedges. The Cyperaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants that are found in many different environments around the world. As a member of this family, Cyperus remotus shares characteristics with other sedges, such as its grass-like leaves and small, spike-like flowers.

intro: Cyperus remotus, also known as the Nut Sedge, is a perennial sedge plant that belongs to the Cyperaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions such as digestive issues and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Cyperus remotus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cyperus remotus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues.
